message:
docs(readme): fix merge_with_options signature/struct/return type (br-nnjnl / fd90.113)

fd90.113: lines 871, 879, 886 (Merge: Advanced Options) had three
calls that all failed to compile, with three compounding errors:

1. Missing right_on slice. crates/fp-join/src/lib.rs:1289 declares
     fn merge_with_options(
         &self,
         other: &DataFrame,
         left_on: &[&str],
         right_on: &[&str],         // <-- README missing this
         how: JoinType,
         options: MergeExecutionOptions,
     ) -> Result<MergedDataFrame, JoinError>;
   The README passed only 4 args after &df2 (no right_on slice).

2. Wrong struct name. README used 'MergeOptions {...}' but the actual
   struct (line 139) is MergeExecutionOptions. No 'MergeOptions' type
   exists — would error E0422.

3. Wrong return type. README binds 'merged' as if it were a DataFrame,
   but the function returns Result<MergedDataFrame, JoinError>.
   MergedDataFrame is a struct with pub index + pub columns fields; a
   DataFrame is reconstructed via DataFrame::new(index, columns).

Fix: add &["key"] for right_on (merging on shared column), rename
MergeOptions → MergeExecutionOptions on all three blocks, and prefix
the section with a note about the MergedDataFrame return contract.

Pure README change. No code impact.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-nmvv","title":"pandas: groupby.cumcount(ascending=False) parity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T23:57:17.779721621Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T00:03:20.260039441Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T00:03:20.258349131Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-nnjnl","title":"fd90.113: README merge_with_options uses 4 args not 5 + wrong struct name MergeOptions vs MergeExecutionOptions","description":"fd90.74-112 README audit. Lines 871, 879, 886 (Merge: Advanced Options section) show three calls to merge_with_options that all fail to compile:

  let merged = df1.merge_with_options(&df2, &["key"], JoinType::Inner,
      MergeOptions { ... })?;

Three errors:

1. Wrong arg count. crates/fp-join/src/lib.rs:1289 declares
     fn merge_with_options(
         &self,
         other: &DataFrame,
         left_on: &[&str],
         right_on: &[&str],   // <-- README missing this
         how: JoinType,
         options: MergeExecutionOptions,
     ) -> Result<MergedDataFrame, JoinError>;
   The README passes only 4 args (no right_on slice).

2. Wrong struct name. README uses 'MergeOptions {...}' but the actual
   struct (line 139) is MergeExecutionOptions. No struct called
   'MergeOptions' exists — would error E0422.

3. Wrong result type binding. README binds via '?' as if it returned
   Result<DataFrame, ...>, but actually returns
   Result<MergedDataFrame, JoinError>. MergedDataFrame is a wrapper
   that requires .into() or accessor calls to extract the underlying
   DataFrame.

Fix: All three examples need: (a) split single &["key"] into two
identical slices for left_on/right_on, (b) MergeOptions →
MergeExecutionOptions, (c) note the MergedDataFrame return type.
Or alternatively use the simpler merge(on, how) variant for examples
that don't actually need the options struct.

Pure README change.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T10:48:24.768221860Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T10:50:05.397209121Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T10:50:05.394483981Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-no1t","title":"pandas: fp-frame DataFrameGroupBy.transform callable-closure parity","description":"py signature:
df.groupby('k').transform(lambda s: (s - s.mean()) / s.std()) -> DataFrame

Current DataFrameGroupBy.transform only takes func_name: &str (calls apply_agg_func then broadcasts). Pandas supports passing a callable that receives a Series (per-group, per-column) and returns a same-length Series. Add transform_fn<F>(func: F) where F: Fn(&Series) -> Result<Series, FrameError>, iterating each value column and each group, invoking the closure, and reassembling in original row order.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T12:51:02.737433381Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T14:16:10.671278365Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T14:16:10.669428772Z","close_reason":"Implemented callable groupby transform parity in commit 7f8d8f6","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-npki","title":"[MEDIUM] hygiene: no cargo-machete / cargo-udeps in CI — unused deps inflate compile time + supply-chain surface","description":"Unused-dependency hygiene audit.

**Observation.** grep for `machete|udeps|unused-dep` in .github/workflows/ci.yml → zero matches. No `cargo machete` or `cargo +nightly udeps` step anywhere.

Workspace has substantial deps (arrow 58, parquet 58, calamine 0.34, rusqlite 0.39, chrono 0.4, chrono-tz 0.10, bumpalo 3.20, bytes 1.11, rust_xlsxwriter 0.94, serde 1.0.228, serde_json 1.0.149, regex 1.12, thiserror 2.0.18, unicode-casefold 0.2, raptorq 2.0, sha2 0.11, serde_yaml 0.9.34-deprecated, proptest 1.11, tempfile 3.27) and recently gained more through the SQL backend epic.

**Why this matters today.**
- `cargo build` compiles every declared dep, not just the used ones. Unused deps inflate compile time + binary size.
- Dependency audit (br-36qc cargo-audit) surfaces CVEs in ALL deps — including unused ones. A vuln in an unused dep is a false alarm that distracts from real findings.
- Supply-chain attack surface: every dep is a potential vector. Fewer deps = smaller surface.
- The library-updater sweeps (UPGRADE_LOG.md) touch every dep, even unused ones. Cleanup would narrow future sweep scope.

**Concrete risk example.** `serde_yaml = "0.9.34+deprecated"` sits in fp-conformance for YAML parity-gate parsing. If conformance gates drop YAML support, serde_yaml becomes an unused-but-still-compiled dep. We currently have no signal that would surface this.

**Severity.** MEDIUM — compound cost is real but gradual. No shipped-code impact today. Force-multiplier for cleaner supply-chain posture + faster compiles. Honest calibration: not HIGH.

**Fix shape:**

1. CI job (either in `checks` or the post-ffhs split):
   ```yaml
   - name: Install cargo-machete
     run: cargo install cargo-machete --locked

   - name: Check unused deps
     run: cargo machete
   ```

2. Alternative / complementary: `cargo +nightly udeps` — catches unused deps more thoroughly but requires nightly (we're already on nightly per br-1d9y).

3. Allowlist for legitimate declare-but-don't-directly-use cases:
   `.cargo-machete.toml`:
   ```toml
   [package]
   ignored = []  # populate if any false positives surface
   ```

4. Pre-commit hook (optional): run `cargo machete` on affected Cargo.toml only when those files change in staged diff.

5. One-time cleanup pass after the bead lands: remove any deps cargo-machete flags. Expect ~0-3 actual removals.

Blast radius: 1 CI step (~8 lines), 1 optional allowlist file (~5 lines), zero source changes on the happy path, minor Cargo.toml trimming if real unused deps surface.

Prerequisites:
- br-ffhs (CI job splitting) — natural new job ("hygiene").
- br-1d9y (nightly pin) — needed for `cargo udeps` alternative.

**Related:**
- br-36qc (cargo-audit) — same CI family; both are dependency hygiene. Bundle in a single "security + hygiene" job.
- br-hg60 (secret scan) — same family.
- UPGRADE_LOG.md (lib-updater workflow) — this bead's output narrows future sweep scope.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:43:18.198543899Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-23T16:34:30.733874951Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T16:34:30.731186655Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","dependencies","hygiene","review"]}
